fault_hypothesis: Lines 71-72 contain a spurious post-processing step that unconditionally pops the last element when both inputs share the same tail value, incorrectly removing a valid duplicate element from the merged result.

spec.expr: not (merged__1 and left and right and merged__1[-1] == left[-1] == right[-1])

constraints.intent: The condition triggering the pop should never be true for valid merged inputs where duplicates are expected to be preserved.
After the merge, the result length must always equal the sum of input lengths; popping an element violates this invariant.
At initialization merged__1 must be empty before any elements are appended.
The returned merged list must contain exactly as many elements as both inputs combined.
